danger_signals.visual_form: Vertical environmental design with ascending routes
danger_signals.meaning: Headquarters of Lawbreakers/Governors faction; hostile territory

safety_signals.meaning: Mentor figure offering Force power upgrades; provides lore and guidance

safety_signals.visual_form: Previously-visited areas marked for fast travel via Force power
safety_signals.meaning: Safe retreat points; accessible through supernatural ability

safety_signals.visual_form: Beach vacation location at game start
safety_signals.meaning: Home/safe zone; represents normal civilian life outside danger

faction_indicators.faction: S.A.B.R.E. (Special Assault Brigade for Real Emergencies)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Secret Squadron Sabre-Toothed Tigers emblem (mentioned in JP intro, removed in US)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military briefing location; Commander Hasslewine/Winestuff command center (JP version only)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Royal Medallions/King Medals (six distributed among governors; seventh controls bomb)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Governor sprites guarding medallion locations throughout routes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Demonhead mountain fortress; Route 4 as enemy headquarters; barricaded positions

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Doomsday Bomb/Dead End Bomb (ancient alien technology)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Alien voices/presence in final confrontation; ultimate antagonist force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien technology integrated into Demonhead facility; final boss arena

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Beach setting with Mary; normal platformer aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Billy operates normally with handgun; open exploration allowed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortress/mountain terrain; Governor enemy sprites present in routes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat required; platforming challenges increase; Force powers obtainable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: MadGazer/Boss sprite appears; mental discomfort indicators (gameplay disruption)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Michael (ally) becomes mind-controlled enemy; demonic presence corrupts environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Countdown timer displayed; alien technology visuals dominate final area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Timed puzzle requiring medallion placement; race-against-clock mechanics

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Good ending - bomb defused, aliens departing
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ending scene showing escape with Hermit; reunion with Mary; credits roll (JP only)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs congratulate player; alien race announces withdrawal; humanity gains autonomy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mushroom cloud/explosion graphics; Earth destruction montage (JP version shows mountain/UFO imagery)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game over state; philosophical text about humanity's self-destruction (JP version includes thematic narration)

environmental_storytelling.example: Six medallions held by Seven Governors creates puzzle-logic: each stage route contains governor with medallion; collecting all six (plus seventh from defeated demon) required for bomb defuse
Shopkeeper design differs between JP (goofy, stubbled face) and US (serious, mustached face) signaling localization intent and tone shift
Over 40 available routes across Demonhead foothills allow non-linear exploration; stage selection indicates faction control of territory and danger scaling
Commander Hasslewine briefing scene establishes S.A.B.R.E. authority; Route 4 conversation between MadGazer and controlled Professor Plum reveals mind-control plot mid-game
JP version includes UFO animation on title screen; removed in US version, foreshadowing alien involvement (later payoff in ending)

environmental_storytelling.technique: Force power acquisition as environmental gating
environmental_storytelling.example: Shrine/Force collection mechanics tied to Hermit apprenticeship; unlocking shrinking, teleportation, flight, healing signals progression and world-state changes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard platforming and shooting; limited combat effectiveness
Access to shrinking, teleportation, flight, healing, invincibility; can navigate otherwise inaccessible areas and bypass obstacles
Soul ascends to heaven; respawn mechanic indicates spiritual rather than physical death
Provides information about grieving sprite and Hermit location; acts as helpful guide
Becomes hostile enemy; lures player toward Magic Stone trap; serves demon's strategic interests
Controls Lawbreakers; commands Governor forces; distributes medallions to prevent bomb defuse
Killed and stripped of medallion by MadGazer; power transferred to superior threat
Attempts mind control of Billy; sends mental discomfort waves; orchestrates events through Michael proxy
Direct combat threat; requires Sword of Apollo to defeat; takes control of narrative threat level

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Passive observers during bomb defuse sequence
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Maintains countdown timer; monitors player progress; ready to activate apocalypse weapon
Announces disdain for humanity; declares permanent withdrawal from Earth; ceases interference
Creates Doomsday Bomb under demon/alien control; remains threat-relevant through technology
Escapes captors independently; reveals himself as separate entity from bomb control; provides narrative closure
Guard assigned medallion routes; present combat encounter in specific stage locations; represent faction military presence
Teaches Force powers; shares lore about demon and Sword of Apollo; offers apprenticeship at ending; represents mentor archetype

notes: Clash at Demonhead employs a metroidvania structure where world-state progression is tied to NPC affiliation and Force power acquisition. Danger signals derive primarily from faction presence (Governors) and singular threat escalation (demon > alien race). Regional differences (JP intro with military briefing, UFO imagery, staff credits) significantly impact narrative world-state communication—US version strips explicit faction context. The game communicates threat through NPC behavioral corruption (Michael's mind control) rather than visual aesthetic shifts, requiring player attention to dialogue and character relationships. Boss entity (MadGazer) serves as the true narrative pivot point, moving conflict from political (Lawbreakers vs. S.A.B.R.E.) to existential (humanity vs. alien judgment). Final world-state bifurcates on player success: either alien withdrawal/human autonomy (good ending) or planetary destruction with thematic narration (bad ending, JP version). Environmental storytelling relies on stage exploration revealing medallion distribution, NPC locations, and Hermit power-granting, creating a puzzle-logic manifestation of faction control and supernatural threat presence.
